name: Angels and celestial beings
shortDescription: Angels are spiritual beings in Christianity who serve as messengers and agents of God. They are often depicted as benevolent and powerful, playing various roles in divine intervention and cosmic order.
description: In Christian theology, angels are considered created spiritual beings, distinct from humans and possessing a higher order of existence. They serve as intermediaries between God and humanity, carrying out divine commands and offering guidance. The Bible describes a hierarchy of angels, including archangels like Michael, Gabriel, and Raphael, each with specific roles and responsibilities. Angels are often depicted as winged figures, symbolizing their ability to travel between the heavenly and earthly realms. They appear in numerous biblical narratives, such as delivering messages to Mary (the Annunciation) and protecting believers. The concept of guardian angels, assigned to individuals for protection and guidance, is also prevalent. While generally benevolent, some angels, led by Lucifer (Satan), rebelled against God and were cast out of heaven, becoming demons or fallen angels. Angels are integral to Christian cosmology, representing God's power, justice, and mercy, and playing a significant role in the ongoing battle between good and evil. Their existence affirms the spiritual dimension of reality and the active involvement of God in the world.
